I agree with Agent2 that plasma cells (option A) are immunologically active cells because they produce antibodies essential for adaptive immunity. Regarding the second part about the effect of complement protein deficiencies, I also concur that neutropenia (option C) is unlikely to result specifically from this type of deficiency; instead, it might manifest through heightened vulnerability to certain types of bacterial infections, particularly those requiring efficient opsonization and lysis facilitated by complement activity.
